steps.title: Convert given rates to calories per hour
steps.content:
The problem gives exercise rates in 30-minute intervals. To work with hours, multiply each rate by 2:
Hiking: $200 \times 2 = 400$ calories/hour
Bicycling: $150 \times 2 = 300$ calories/hour

steps.title: Calculate calories from 1 hour of bicycling
steps.content:
The adult has already completed 1 hour of bicycling:
$$1 \text{ hour} \times 300 \text{ calories/hour} = 300 \text{ calories}$$

steps.title: Set up and solve for hiking hours
steps.content:
Find the remaining calories needed, then solve for hiking time. Remaining calories: $1,900 - 300 = 1,600$. Let $h$ = hours of hiking. Then:
$$400h = 1,600$$
$$h = \frac{1,600}{400} = 4$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
